texto: In metric, one milliliter of water occupies one cubic centimeter, weighs one gram, and requires one calorie of energy to heat up by one degree centigrade—which is 1 percent of the difference between its freezing point and its boiling point. An amount of hydrogen weighing the same amount has exactly one mole of atoms in it. Whereas in the American system, the answer to ‘How much energy does it take to boil a room-temperature gallon of water?’ is ‘Go fuck yourself,’ because you can’t directly relate any of those quantities.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Josh Bazell que provoca passagem do tempo e prioridades. Ao questionar “In metric, one milliliter of water occupies one cubic centimeter, weighs one gram, and requires o…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre autoestima, america, americans, com foco em americans e measurement-units.

texto: Like most people raised on American movies, I have poor access to my emotions, but can banter like a motherfucker.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Josh Bazell: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a familia. O trecho “Like most people raised on American movies, I have poor access to my emotions, but can banter lik…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, movies, snark pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de snark.
